reason: The statement applies because the text fragment describes the story of Balaam, a prominent figure in the Hebrew Bible, who was summoned by Balak, the king of Moab, to curse the people of Israel who had come out from Egypt. Balaam's interaction with God and his refusal to curse the Israelites despite Balak's request align with the idea of a prominent Hebrew (Israelite) moving to Egypt for safety reasons as the land of Moab was not tenable due to the conflict and curse attempt described in the text.
